[
  {
    "idea": "Weekly surge in Google Trends for 'energy bill shock' signals bearish XLE",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly data for 'energy bill shock'",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "A rise in searches for energy bill shock indicates consumer stress from rising energy costs, potentially reducing discretionary spending and pressuring energy sector stocks.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLE",
      "rationale": "Consumer stress related to energy costs weighs on demand and sentiment in the energy sector."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "Enter short when Google Trends index for 'energy bill shock' increases by more than 15% week-over-week",
      "exit": "Exit after 3 weeks or when the index falls below the 4-week moving average",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Energy prices fluctuate frequently causing repeated consumer stress search spikes."
  },
